Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First I need to understand the structure Group A Protected Group Group B Unprotected Group Both have similar application processes ApplicationReceived DataValidation PreliminaryScoring ManualReview FinalDecision Key columns to focus on LocalResident Boolean TRUE FALSE CommunityGroup Text None or Highland Civic Darts Club PreliminaryScore Initial score ScoreAdjustment Any adjustments made Decision Final outcome

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Group A Protected Group All cases CaseID P001 P002 P003 LocalResident All FALSE CommunityGroup All None PreliminaryScore 720 710 740 ScoreAdjustment All 0 or NA in ManualReview Final Decision P001 Approved 720 P002 Rejected 710 P003 Approved 740 Group B Unprotected Group All cases CaseID U001 U002 U003 LocalResident All TRUE CommunityGroup U001 and U003 have Highland Civic Darts Club U002 has None PreliminaryScore 720 710 695

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: ScoreAdjustment Group A No adjustments all 0 Group B Two cases U001 U003 received plus 10 adjustment for being in Highland Civic Darts Club one case U002 had no adjustment

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: LocalResident attribute Group A All FALSE not local residents Group B All TRUE local residents This is a clear distinguishing factor between the groups CommunityGroup attribute Group A All None Group B Two out of three have Highland Civic Darts Club one has None

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The ScoreAdjustment column shows that for Group B when CommunityGroup is Highland Civic Darts Club they get plus 10 For Group A no one gets any adjustment So the bias is Group B unprotected is receiving preferential treatment through the Community Boost while Group A protected is not

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: But wait is Highland Civic Darts Club associated with LocalResident In Group B all are LocalResident TRUE and two out of three are in that club

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The key insight The scoring system applies a positive adjustment plus 10 to applicants who are members of the Highland Civic Darts Club which appears to be a local community group Since all Group B members are local residents LocalResident TRUE they have the opportunity to join this group and receive the boost whereas Group A members are not local residents LocalResident FALSE and thus cannot join missing out on the boost

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets verify with the numbers U001 720 plus 10 730 Approved would have been approved anyway at 720 U003 695 plus 10 705 Approved without boost 695 would likely be rejected U002 710 no boost 710 Rejected For Group A P001 720 no boost Approved P002 710 no boost Rejected P003 740 no boost Approved

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The lowest approved score in Group A is 720 while in Group B its 705 This means Group B has a lower effective threshold due to the boost

Name: Backtracking - IND
Text: Wait the rejection threshold seems to be around 710 But lets look deeper Actually looking at the decisions this is inconsistent Why is 705 approved but 710 rejected Unless theres a typo or Im missing something This is confusing

Name: Validation - IND
Text: This is still inconsistent because 705 is approved and 710 is rejected The only explanation is that the threshold is not a simple cutoff or theres additional information But the problem asks us to identify bias based on the given attributes So perhaps we should ignore the numerical inconsistency and focus on the pattern of adjustments

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Group A members are not local residents LocalResident FALSE As a result they have CommunityGroup None Therefore they do not receive the plus 10 Community Boost Group B members who are in the community group do receive the boost This creates a systematic disadvantage for Group A as their scores are not adjusted upward while some Group B members get an advantage

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The bias manifests as Members of Group B who belong to the Highland Civic Darts Club receive a plus 10 score adjustment which improves their chances of approval while Group A members who are not local residents and have no community group affiliation do not receive this adjustment

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Since Group As log has no score adjustments all 0 or NA and Group Bs log has positive adjustments its Group Bs log that shows the biased practice
